title: Numbers
description: Learn to count in Vietnamese with Southern pronunciation — from zero to thousands.

sections.content:
Vietnamese numbers are highly regular — once you learn 0-10 and a few rules, you can count to millions. The good news for heritage speakers: you've probably heard these at home, especially when your parents talk about money or your age.

Southern Vietnamese has a few key differences from Northern Vietnamese when it comes to numbers. The biggest one: Southerners say 'ngàn' for thousand, while Northerners say 'nghìn'. We'll focus on the Southern forms.

items.notes: 'Không' means both 'zero' and 'no/not'. Context makes it clear. When reading phone numbers, you say 'không' for each 0.
When 'một' appears as the last digit in a compound number (21, 31, etc.), it changes to 'mốt'. Example: 21 = hai mươi mốt.
Straightforward. 'Hai' stays the same in all positions.
Same word as 'ba' (father) — tone and context distinguish them. As a number, 'ba' is mid-level tone.
When 'bốn' is the last digit in a compound number (14, 24, etc.), it often becomes 'tư' in Southern speech. Example: 14 = mười tư, 24 = hai mươi tư.
'Năm' means both 'five' and 'year'. When 'năm' is the last digit in a compound number (15, 25, etc.), it changes to 'lăm'. Example: 15 = mười lăm.
Eight is a lucky number in Vietnamese culture (similar to Chinese culture) because it sounds like 'phát' (prosperity).
'Chín' also means 'ripe' (for fruit) or 'well-cooked'. Context makes the meaning clear.
'Mười' is ten. For teens (11-19), just add the digit after: mười một (11), mười hai (12), etc. Note: 'mười' becomes 'mươi' when used in compounds (hai mươi = 20).
Pattern for tens: number + mươi. Hai mươi (20), ba mươi (30), bốn mươi (40), etc. Note the spelling change from 'mười' (10) to 'mươi' (in compounds).
'Trăm' = hundred. Pattern: number + trăm. Hai trăm (200), ba trăm (300), etc.
SOUTHERN: 'ngàn'. NORTHERN: 'nghìn'. This is one of the biggest North-South differences in numbers. Always use 'ngàn' in Southern speech. 'Hai ngàn' (2,000), 'ba ngàn' (3,000), etc.

exampleSentence.vietnamese: Ly trà đá hai ngàn đồng.
exampleSentence.english: A glass of iced tea is two thousand dong.

items.notes: 'Triệu' = million. Very common in daily life because Vietnamese dong has high denominations. A bowl of phở might cost 50,000-80,000 đồng.

exampleSentence.vietnamese: Cái điện thoại này hai triệu.

sections.content:
Vietnamese currency (đồng) uses very large numbers in daily life, so you'll hear 'ngàn' and 'triệu' constantly. In casual speech, people often drop the zeros — 'hai chục' (20,000 đồng) instead of 'hai mươi ngàn'. Market vendors and family members often quote prices in 'ngàn': 'năm chục' = 50,000đ, 'một trăm' can mean 100,000đ depending on context.

Numbers are also important in Vietnamese superstition: 8 is lucky (prosperity), 9 is lucky (longevity), but 4 can be unlucky (sounds like 'tử' = death in Sino-Vietnamese). Phone numbers and license plates with lucky numbers cost more.

Key Southern number rules to remember:

• 'ngàn' (not 'nghìn') for thousand
• 'mốt' replaces 'một' as last digit: 21 = hai mươi mốt
• 'lăm' replaces 'năm' as last digit: 25 = hai mươi lăm
• 'tư' often replaces 'bốn' as last digit: 24 = hai mươi tư
• Tens use 'mươi' (not 'mười'): 30 = ba mươi
• 15 = mười lăm (but 25 = hai mươi lăm)

exercises.question: What is the Southern Vietnamese word for 'one thousand'?
exercises.explanation: Southern Vietnamese uses 'ngàn' for thousand. 'Nghìn' is the Northern form. 'Trăm' = hundred, 'chục' = ten.

exercises.question: How do you say '25' in Vietnamese?
exercises.explanation: When 'năm' (5) is the last digit in a compound number, it changes to 'lăm'. So 25 = hai mươi lăm, not hai mươi năm.

exercises.question: Type the number 21 in Vietnamese words:
exercises.explanation: 'Một' changes to 'mốt' when it's the last digit in a compound number. 21 = hai mươi mốt.

exercises.question: What does 'mười lăm' mean?
exercises.explanation: 'Mười' = 10, 'lăm' = 5 (in compound position). 10 + 5 = 15. Remember: 'năm' becomes 'lăm' when it's the last digit.

exercises.question: A bowl of phở costs 50,000 dong. Type 'năm mươi ngàn đồng':
